claims.snippet: This function is continuous on $[0,1]$ so we can safely integrate it on this interval.

issues.title: False continuity or safe-integrability claim
issues.snippet: This function is continuous on $[0,1]$ so we can safely integrate it on this interval.
issues.explanation: The function is not continuous on the stated closed interval, so the paper's claim that it can be safely integrated there is not justified.
issues.fix: Either change the interval to avoid singularities, remove the claim, or rewrite the passage as an improper-integral discussion.
issues.evidence: Continuous domain over the reals is Union(Interval.open(-oo, 1), Interval.open(1, oo)), which does not contain the full interval Interval(0, 1).
